title: DevOps Engineer
description: **DevOps Engineer** **Contract** **Hybrid(On site 3 days Dublin)** We're looking for an experienced DevSecOps Engineer with a strong focus on cloud infrastructure, automation, CI/CD, and security Key Skills Required * Strong experience with **Microsoft Azure** * Advanced **Terraform** skills for Infrastructure as Code * Hands\-on experience with **GitHub Actions** for CI/CD pipeline development * Experience with **Docker** , **Kubernetes** , and **Azure Container Apps** * Knowledge of **JFrog Artifactory** * Experience with **Snyk** and **SonarQube** for security scanning and code quality * Monitoring and observability using **Datadog, Azure Monitor, Prometheus, or Grafana** * Scripting skills in **Python, Bash, or PowerShell** * Experience working in Agile environments using **Jira** - Nice to Have * OpenShift experience * Jenkins or Azure DevOps knowledge * Relevant certifications (Azure, Terraform, CKA/CKAD, DevSecOps) **Apply via LinkedIn or email your CV to katie.cowzer@realtime.jobs**

title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: DevSecOps Engineer
description: **Company Overview** Holafly is a high\-growth scale\-up revolutionising how businesses and travellers connect to the internet abroad. Since 2018, we’ve empowered travellers in over 200 destinations worldwide with secure and reliable eSIM solutions. With a team of 500\+ professionals across multiple countries, we are scaling globally to support travellers with seamless, unlimited data connectivity. We’re not just connecting people—we’re enabling freedom and peace of mind, ensuring our users stay connected from the second they land, wherever their journey takes them. **The Role** You will be the architect of our cloud foundations, ensuring security is baked into our DNA rather than bolted on as an afterthought. Your work directly enables our engineering teams to scale safely, protecting the connectivity of millions of global travellers. **Key Responsibilities** * Design and operate the GCP cloud platform foundations, owning the full lifecycle of infrastructure and security. * Automate everything via Terraform and Ansible, eliminating "snowflake" servers and manual console configurations. * Maintain and scale GKE clusters, managing workloads, network policies, and cluster lifecycles. * Strengthen our security posture by implementing least\-privilege IAM, image scanning, and SOPS\-based secret management. * Optimise CI/CD pipelines using Cloud Build and GitHub to ensure high\-quality, secure code deployments. * Enhance observability and incident response through OpenTelemetry and OpenSearch to ensure 24/7 platform reliability. **Qualifications** * Proven experience managing Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and Kubernetes (GKE) at scale. * Expertise in Infrastructure as Code (IaC) specifically using Terraform. * Strong proficiency in Python or Bash for high\-level automation and tooling. * Deep understanding of container security, including Docker and artifact vulnerability scanning. * A "security\-first" mindset with experience in secret management and supply\-chain hygiene. **Nice To Have** * Experience with OpenTelemetry or OpenSearch instrumentation. * Familiarity with SonarQube for automated code quality gates. * Previous experience in high\-growth scale\-ups or the travel\-tech industry. **Benefits \& Perks** * 25 Days of PTO * 5% pension contribution * € 3000 for Medical/Health coverage * Self development budget of $ 500 During a trip to Thailand, Pedro and Lidia, the founders of Holafly, experienced the frustration of not having an internet connection. Inspired by their own experiences, they came up with a simple but brilliant idea: why not allow travellers to buy data plans for any destination from their home country? Our passion for providing easy and convenient connectivity to travellers around the world drives everything we do. We love our work and we do it with pride. Stay connected wherever you go.

title: Devops Engineer -  DDT
description: **Overview** Susquehanna is a global quantitative trading firm powered by scientific rigor, curiosity, and innovation. Our culture is intellectually driven and highly collaborative, bringing together researchers, engineers, and traders to design and deploy impactful strategies in our systematic trading environment. To meet the unique challenges of global markets, Susquehanna applies machine learning and advanced quantitative research to vast datasets in order to uncover actionable insights and build effective strategies. By uniting deep market expertise with cutting\-edge technology, we excel in solving complex problems and pushing boundaries together. A DevOps Engineer in the Data Driven Trading (DDT) team ensures the smooth development, delivery, and ongoing operation of the software systems the desk depends on. We partner with developers, quantitative researchers, and traders in Dublin and across the globe to deliver reliable, efficient software at scale. The role focuses on production stability, CI/CD reliability, robust tooling, and enabling engineering teams to build and ship software efficiently across a highly distributed environment. You will work closely with global engineering and DevOps resources to operate and improve the systems that are a critical dependency for the trading desk. The primary focus of the role is to improve the reliability and operational effectiveness of Quantitative Research tools and systems, while streamlining new capabilities that unlock value for the desk. The successful candidate thrives in a fast\-paced, distributed engineering environment and is comfortable participating in critical parts of the delivery pipeline. They combine strong operational discipline with a proactive approach to automation and continuous improvement, and are curious about applying AI to reduce repetitive work. They communicate clearly with developers and traders, take initiative in diagnosing complex issues, and consistently drive problems to resolution. A successful engineer in this role is someone who not only solves problems, but also strengthens the systems, tools, and processes that prevent those problems from recurring. Job Responsibilities * Member of the DevOps team within the DDT trading desk * Responsible for the operations of critical systems used by the desk * Troubleshoot and resolve production incidents and change requests within defined SLAs, performing root\-cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes to prevent recurrence * Manage and support production deployments across all development lifecycle stages, ensuring reliable data retrieval, retention, and compliance with data policies * Maintain and improve a large, distributed set of CI/CD pipelines — monitoring pipeline health, investigating execution failures, and driving remediation * Extend existing pipelines and build ad\-hoc or pattern\-based pipelines to support new project requirements * Identify recurring issues and implement automation or structural improvements to eliminate them * Update and maintain toolchains, build dependencies, and related infrastructure; apply retention policies across relevant systems and environments * Drive change that improves uptime and reliability of systems, including configuration and development work where appropriate * Act as an ambassador for excellence by ensuring change into the system is of high quality and fits the DDT DevOps global operating model * Conduct daily operational checks, triage issues, and coordinate timely resolution * Contribute to the evolution of DevOps practices, reliability engineering, and automation strategy * Member of a global follow\-the\-sun team, acting during EMEA hours * Develop expertise in Susquehanna's systems and business **What We're Looking For** * Professional operations mindset coupled with practical development experience to improve systems * Degree in a numerate subject * Evidence of taking accountability for system delivery * 2\+ years professional experience using Python as a scripting language for orchestrating processes and systems; working knowledge of Bash and PowerShell * Hands\-on experience with CI/CD orchestration tools (e.g., TeamCity) * Experience with Apache Airflow or other industry\-standard ETL/orchestration frameworks * Proficiency with enterprise version control platforms (e.g., GitLab) * Experience working in a Linux environment * Understanding and practical application of security models (e.g., RBAC) * Exposure to deployment orchestration platforms (e.g., Octopus Deploy) * Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem\-solving abilities * Ability to self\-manage, self\-motivate, and seek out process improvement opportunities * Strong interpersonal and communication skills that enable effective collaboration in a complex domain * Attention to detail and ability to react to changing priorities * A general interest in quantitative trading **Desirable** * Exposure to cluster/grid management systems (e.g., Slurm) * Experience with dependency/environment management (e.g., Conda) * Familiarity with AI/ML workflow or orchestration tools If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Digital Experience, Brand & Content Strategist - Fuze Health
description: At Fuze Health, we put patients first and tirelessly address the most pressing needs in healthcare. We empower millions to digitally connect with care providers, essential health resources and needed treatments – and enable care providers, employers, health plans and life sciences companies to meaningfully enhance quality, outcomes and value. We are dedicated to helping our partners evolve and modernize to meet emerging patient and marketplace needs. Fuze Health's foundation is built upon the strategic combination of several proven, technology\-powered innovators in the digital health, diagnostics, and pharmacy sectors. Our growing portfolio brings together the capabilities of industry leaders including LetsGetChecked, Truepill, and Alto Pharmacy, to create a distinctive, unified force in healthcare. Together, we have the shared vision, advanced capabilities and talented teams to deliver next\-generation solutions that patients and healthcare partners need today and into the future. The Digital Experience, Brand \& Content Strategist is a senior, cross\-functional role responsible for leading the strategy, planning, and execution of Fuze Health's digital presence, specifically driving a major corporate website redesign initiative. This position bridges the gap between creative storytelling and technical execution to build a high\-performing, user\-centric digital experience. **Fixed Term 6 Month Contract** #### **Website Strategy \& Digital Experience Leadership** * Lead the strategy, planning, and cross\-functional execution of a major corporate website redesign initiative * Define website goals, audience journeys, information architecture, and content strategy aligned to business and brand objectives * Partner with UX, design, development, SEO, and marketing stakeholders to create an intuitive, high\-performing digital experience * Establish governance models, content standards, and scalable site management processes * Ensure digital experiences are optimized for usability, accessibility, conversion, engagement, and performance #### **Brand \& Content Strategy** * Develop and evolve digital brand storytelling frameworks across web and owned channels * Translate company positioning, product strategy, and campaign messaging into clear, compelling digital experiences * Create strategic content plans that support awareness, engagement, demand generation, customer education, and thought leadership * Define content hierarchy, messaging structure, and editorial direction across priority pages and audience segments * Ensure consistency in voice, messaging, and brand expression across digital touchpoints #### **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Partner closely with Brand, Product Marketing, Demand Generation, Creative, and Executive Leadership teams * Collaborate with subject matter experts and stakeholders to develop audience\-focused content and digital experiences * Manage agency and vendor relationships related to website strategy, UX, SEO, content, and development * Serve as a strategic advisor on digital experience best practices and emerging trends #### **Optimization \& Performance** * Monitor and analyze website performance, engagement metrics, SEO performance, and conversion trends * Identify opportunities to improve user experience, content effectiveness, and conversion pathways * Support testing and optimization initiatives across digital experiences * Develop reporting frameworks and actionable insights for leadership **Key Responsibilities** **Required Experience \& Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications: * 6\+ years of experience in digital strategy, content strategy, brand strategy, UX content, or digital marketing * Proven experience leading or managing large\-scale website redesign projects * Strong understanding of digital user experience principles, information architecture, SEO, and conversion optimization * Experience developing strategic messaging and content frameworks for B2B and/or complex healthcare organizations * Strong collaboration and stakeholder management skills across creative, technical, and executive teams * Exceptional communication, writing, and presentation abilities * Experience working with CMS platforms, analytics tools, and digital marketing technologies * Healthcare, health tech, or B2B SaaS experience preferred **Salary Range** €65,680\.00 \- €82,100 At Fuze Health, we are committed to fostering an inclusive environment that celebrates diversity in all its forms. We believe that the diversity of thought, background, and experience strengthens our teams and drives innovation. We are an equal\-opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, color, place of birth, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, military service status, or disability status. Our goal is to ensure that everyone feels valued and empowered to thrive. Fuze Corporate Inc. and its subsidiaries respects your privacy and is committed to protecting your personal information. Please read our Candidate Privacy Notice which explains how we collect, use, disclose, and protect personal information about job applicants during the recruitment and hiring process.

title: Document Control Assistant
description: **About Ethos Quality Control \& Operations** The Ethos Quality Control and Operations Team plays a key role in supporting our engineering teams across all sectors. The team is responsible for maintaining high standards of quality, compliance, document management, and operational efficiency throughout the business. As Ethos continues to grow, we are looking for motivated individuals who are eager to learn and develop their careers within a professional engineering environment. This role offers the opportunity to gain experience across quality systems, document control, project administration, and business operations while working alongside experienced professionals. Our team values accuracy, collaboration, continuous improvement, and attention to detail. We provide training, mentoring, and career development opportunities to help individuals build long\-term careers within the business. **About the job:** As a Document Control Assistant, you will support the Quality Control Team with document management and administrative activities. Working alongside experienced team members, you will assist in maintaining internal records, preparing documentation, supporting quality processes, and ensuring information is organised and accessible. This is an excellent opportunity for an organised and detail\-oriented individual looking to develop a career in document control and quality administration. **About you:** * The candidate must be based in Ireland to be considered for this position. * Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. * Excellent IT and computer literacy skills. * Good written and verbal communication skills. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Positive attitude and willingness to learn. * Strong time management skills and ability to meet deadlines. * Motivated, adaptable, and reliable. * Fluency in English. **Role Requirements** * Assist with maintaining document registers and trackers. * Review documentation for formatting, consistency, completeness, and accuracy. * Support quality management and compliance activities. * Collate reports and supporting documentation. * Assist with general administrative tasks across the wider team. * Provide support to the Document Control team. **Key Knowledge** * Strong Microsoft Office skills including Excel, Word, Outlook, and SharePoint. * Excellent attention to detail and accuracy. * Experience with administrative tasks. * Experience gained through internships, work placements, or office administration roles would be beneficial but is not essential. **Minimum Qualifications** Leaving Certificate or equivalent. Recent graduates, interns, and candidates with administrative experience are encouraged to apply. **Essential Experience** No previous document control experience required. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. **At Ethos, our** ***vision*** **is to:** ***“Be the company people want to work with, and for”*** **Values** Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be **EPIC** \- for our people and our customers. title: Dublin Cabin Crew Opportunities (Dubai Based, Relocation Provided)
description: **Job Purpose** A personality that shines, the ability to adapt to any situation and make people feel at ease. These are a few of the qualities we’re looking for in our cabin crew. As the face of Emirates, you’ll be the person customers turn to for help and direction when they fly with us, so you need to be friendly, observant and able to provide the right support. Being a member of the cabin crew is so much more than a service role \- safety is our highest priority. You’ll need to lead confidently and take control when it comes to managing aircraft services, security, and safety procedures. This comes from the world\-class learning experience our crew receive at our state\-of\-the\-art facility in Dubai. **Qualifications \& Experience** **Here are some other things we look for in our cabin crew:** * You’ve had more than a year’s experience in hospitality/customer service * You have a positive attitude and the natural ability to provide excellent service in a team environment, dealing with people from many cultures * Your minimum qualification is high school graduate (Grade 12\) * You need to be fluent in written and spoken English * You’re at least 160cm tall and can reach 212 cm while standing on tiptoes, to enable you to reach emergency equipment on all aircraft types * No visible tattoos while you’re in Emirates cabin crew uniform (without covering them with bandages or cosmetics) * As Emirates cabin crew, you’ll be based in Dubai and will need to meet the UAE's employment visa requirements Aside from the requirements of the role, you should be determined to always perform to the highest standards, focus on being solution oriented, and be able to manage a demanding work schedule. You should have the ability to deliver an authentic experience to our customers. You’ll be culturally aware and reflect the Emirates personality \- professional, empathetic, progressive, visionary and cosmopolitan. **Salary \& Benefits** Your pay is made up of three components: 1. a fixed basic salary, 2. an hourly pay for operated flights and 3. an overseas meal allowance. Basic salary \= AED 4,980 / month, Flying Pay \= AED 69\.60 / hour based on avg. 80\-100 hours / month, Average Total Pay \= AED 11,244 / month (\~USD 3,100, EUR 2,600 or GBP 2,280\) \* These are approximate numbers for Grade II (Economy Class). Meal allowances for night stops are credited to the salary in arrears the following month. Hotel accommodation as well as transport to and from the airport is provided by the company. Join Emirates as cabin crew and enjoy a competitive, tax\-free salary, flying pay, profit share, and concessional travel. Benefit from comprehensive relocation support to help you settle in Dubai, along with free accommodation and transportation. You will also receive excellent medical, life, and dental insurance, and more. Additionally, your friends and family can enjoy discounted flights, creating lasting memories together.

title: ETF Pricing Analyst
description: Virtu is a leading financial firm that leverages cutting edge technology to deliver liquidity to the global markets and innovative, transparent trading solutions to our clients. As a market maker, Virtu provides deep liquidity that helps to create more efficient markets around the world. Our market structure expertise, broad diversification and execution technology enables us to provide competitive bids and offers in over 19,000 securities, at over 235 venues, in 36 countries worldwide. We are currently looking to enhance our global ETF team. The role will be based in our Dublin office. Virtu Financial Ireland Limited is the European HQ of our proprietary trading business. **THE ROLE** This is an excellent opportunity to become an exchange\-traded fund (ETF) expert. Our ETF Pricing Specialists work closely with our traders and ETF issuers to understand their products and pricing methodologies. They build models to accurately price funds in many assets including commodities, equities, fixed income and emerging asset classes like cryptocurrencies. They deliver on time accurate pricing working in tandem with the trading team to deliver best in class pricing models. **THE CANDIDATE** * Excellent academic background. All subjects welcome but hard sciences preferred (computer science, math, physics, statistics and related fields). * Programming experience is required (Python, SQL, Java or C\+\+ are a plus). * The ability to communicate information precisely. * A person who identifies issues and creates solutions without rigid direction. * Highly motivated and tenacious individual, with a proven ability to take initiative and work independently and within a team. * Team player: authentic, genuine person who is capable of working towards collective goals. * No finance background is necessary. **THE PROCESS** After an initial resume screening, select applicants will receive an online programming\-skills test via email. This is to gain an understanding of candidate coding abilities. The sender will be HackerRank, a third\-party evaluation service we employ, so please be sure to monitor your inbox/spam folder. Following that, successful candidates will be contacted for a phone interview. We look forward to learning more about you. *Virtu Financial is an equal opportunity employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, welcoming you for who you are and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.*

At EY\-Parthenon Strategy, we put our purpose — building a better working world — at the heart of everything we do. title: Electrician

Skills * Experience with DC cabling and rooftop installations * Familiarity with battery storage systems and EV chargers * IT skills for reporting and documentation (Excel, email, mobile apps) Travel \& Flexibility * This role requires regular travel to project sites across Ireland * Overnight stays may be required depending on project location * Company vehicle and accommodation provided where title: Enablement Systems & Automation Specialist
description: LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network, built to create economic opportunity for every member of the global workforce. Our products help people make powerful connections, discover exciting opportunities, build necessary skills, and gain valuable insights every day. We're also committed to providing transformational opportunities for our own employees by investing in their growth. We aspire to create a culture that's built on trust, care, inclusion, and fun – where everyone can succeed. Join us to transform the way the world works. **Job Description** At LinkedIn, our approach to flexible work is centered on trust and optimized for culture, connection, clarity, and the evolving needs of our business. The work location of this role is hybrid, meaning it will be performed both from home and from a LinkedIn office on select days, as determined by the business needs of the team. The Go\-To\-Market Enablement (GTME) team empowers our customer\-facing teams through world\-class enablement experiences and infrastructure. This role sits within Enablement Tech Operations, a function of the GTME Centre of Excellence, and is critical to the operational delivery and performance insights of our global learning programs. **About the Role:** We are looking for an Enablement Systems \& Automation Specialist who thinks in systems, builds with intention, and can navigate the organisational complexity of a large enterprise to turn ideas into operational reality. This is a role with genuine scope and real visibility — one where you will have the opportunity to shape how enablement technology scales across a global GTM organisation. This is not a Learning Management System (LMS) administration role. It is a technical building role — one that treats enablement platforms as parts of a connected operating system and focuses on identifying manual, repeatable work and engineering solutions that eliminate it. The primary focus is automation and integration: from automating the learner experience through curriculum and event management workflows, to expanding proven frameworks and driving operational efficiency across the enablement ecosystem. Equally important is the ability to navigate enterprise complexity. Success in this role requires strong influencing skills to move initiatives through internal governance, access approvals, and policy holder requirements — often without direct authority over the teams involved. ETOps is a function that values craft, rigour, and momentum. If you are a learning and development technologist who wants to build things that matter and leave a real mark on how a world\-class enablement organisation operates, this is the role for you. **Responsibilities:** Automation \& Systems Building * Design, build, and maintain automations, integrations, and AI\-driven workflows that reduce manual effort and scale enablement operations * Automate the learner experience through curriculum and event management workflows, reducing manual effort and improving consistency across lines of business * Identify high\-impact opportunities for process elimination, not just process improvement * Evaluate and operationalise AI tools that improve how ETOps and the broader enablement function works Stakeholder \& Governance Navigation * Partner with Information Security, IT, engineering, and system owners to secure access, approvals, and technical support for initiatives * Navigate governance requirements and organisational processes to move complex, cross\-functional work forward * Influence stakeholders and maintain momentum in environments with competing priorities * Persist through ambiguity and structural complexity to deliver business outcomes Platform Operations \& Reporting * Support the configuration, governance, and integrity of learning technology and enablement tools across the stack * Contribute to reporting infrastructure and data flows that surface enablement performance insights for stakeholders and leadership * Build and maintain technical documentation, system governance standards, and knowledge resources * Scale and socialise best practices across multiple workstreams and global stakeholders **Qualifications** **Basic Qualifications** * 2\+ years of experience as a learning and development technologist, enablement technology specialist, or in a related learning operations or systems role * Demonstrated experience building automations or integrations using tools such as Power Automate, Zapier, APIs, or equivalent * Experience working with learning technology and enablement tools, including Learning Management Systems (LMS), and comparable enterprise platforms **Preferred Qualifications** * Strong documentation, analytical, and problem\-solving skills * Demonstrated experience implementing, developing, and scaling automations through learning technology and enablement platforms * Familiarity with AI tooling, prompt engineering, or agentic workflows * Demonstrated ability to influence cross\-functional stakeholders and deliver outcomes without direct authority * Experience navigating security, governance, and access approval processes in a large enterprise environment * Comfort operating in ambiguity and driving progress with limited oversight * Experience supporting large\-scale enablement or sales performance programs **Suggested Skills** * Workflow Automation * Systems Integration * Learning Management Systems (LMS) * AI Tool Adoption \& Operationalisation * Stakeholder Management \& Influence * Data Analysis \& Reporting **Additional Information** **Global Data Privacy Notice and Compliance Posters for Job Candidates** Please use this link to access documents that provide information about how LinkedIn handles the personal data of employees and job applicants, as well as the E\-Verify Participation Notice and the Department of Justice Immigrant and Employee Rights Section Right to Work posters: https://www.linkedin.com/legal/candidate\-portal.

title: Fluvial Geomorphologist - Hydromorphologist
description: **Overview** ------------ We are looking for a Hydromorphologist / Geomorphologist to join our team and work at the forefront of helping address the challenges of maintaining, creating or restoring water courses to/in good ecological status, ensuring that their form and function is incorporated into and/or protected from new infrastructure development, as well as restored and recovered from previous interventions that may have physically degraded them, e.g. channelisation, presence of weirs, culverts etc. We are undertaking an increasing number and range of important and exciting projects that involve assessing the current state of water courses and designing and developing methods and proposals for their restoration, maintenance or creation, dependent on the type of project. We are working for a range of clients such as water companies, community groups and other government and private sector entities and authorities. Building on this success, we are growing our team, and this growth could include you. You could help us to win and deliver work across our extensive client portfolio and enhance our reputation for excellence. As hydromorphologist, you will have experience of geomorphology or hydromorphology related project tasks in Ireland, the UK, Europe and/or internationally. You will work closely with our technical specialists who are based in our established teams across Ireland, both the Republic and in the North, and in the UK. We have a friendly and approachable team with a strong reputation for delivering excellence with multiple clients. **Your role** ------------- * With a degree in science, engineering or mathematics. * A post\-graduate qualification in hydromorphology or geomorphology, or a related subject, would be desirable. * You will have hands\-on, in\-the\-field, practical experience of hydromorphological assessment of river channels and water courses. * You will have experience as a hydromorphologist. * You will preferably understand fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology in relation to its role in determining Good Ecological Status/Potential under the Water Framework Directive. * Your experience will include geomorphology and hydromorphology, for example work on natural flood management, nature restoration, channel creation, maintenance and management. * You will have experience of delivering parts of, or whole, project tasks, such as data collection, data and information analysis, interpretation and drawing succinct conclusions and recommendations. * You will have good numerical, analytical, report\-writing and presentational skills and an eye for detail. * You will be highly motivated and proactive and enjoy solving challenging fluvial geomorphological problems within a high achieving team environment. * You will be able to communicate effectively with your colleagues to support collaborative working. * You will have valid full, clean, Irish driving license. **About you** ------------- * Demonstrable knowledge and practical use of one or more of the following would be an advantage: + River Hydromorphology Assessment Technique (RHAT) survey method for river water bodies. + The Lake Morphology and Modification Assessment System (Lake MImAS) for lakes. + Hydromorphological Quality Index (HQI) for transitional and coastal (TraC) waters, and/or Morphological Quality Index for Ireland (Quinlan and Mockler, 2020\). * Ability to survey, assess, appraise and report on the state of fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology in water courses and water bodies. * Knowledge and understanding of key fluvial geomorphology / hydromorphology issues and their drivers, preferably in an Irish, UK or European context. * Experience of writing funding proposals, costing consultancy tenders and preparing technical and financial aspects of tenders is desirable. **Rewards \& benefits** ----------------------- Explore the rewards and benefits that help you thrive – at every stage of your life and your career. Enjoy competitive salaries and a brilliant range of health, wellbeing, financial and lifestyle benefits. Make the most of a myriad of opportunities for training and professional development to grow your skills and expertise. And combine our hybrid working culture, international remote working opportunities, and flexible holiday allowances to balance a great job and fulfilling personal life. Be rewarded. **About AtkinsRéalis** ---------------------- We're AtkinsRéalis, a world\-class engineering services and nuclear organization. We connect people, data and technology to transform the world's'infrastructure and energy systems. Together, with our industry partners and clients, and our global team of consultants, designers, engineers and project managers, we can change the world. We're committed to leading our clients across our various end markets to engineer a better future for our planet and its people. **Additional information** -------------------------- We are committed to creating a culture where everyone feels that they belong \- a place where we can all be ourselves, thrive and develop to be the best we can be. So, we offer a range of family friendly, inclusive employment policies, flexible working arrangements and employee resource groups to support all employees. As an Equal Opportunities Employer, we value applications from all backgrounds, cultures and ability. **\#LI\-UKI**

title: Forward Deployed Engineer
description: **At Poka, we’re transforming how frontline teams operate, learn, and solve problems.** **Our connected worker platform is purpose\-built for manufacturing, empowering frontline workers to share knowledge, collaborate in real\-time, and drive continuous improvement. Think of it as the missing digital link, bringing the human interface into the stack of the Smart Factory, generating the right data and insights that drive entirely new scales of process improvements.** **We’re proud to support global leaders like Nestlé, Bosch, Mars, and Danone in their digital transformation journeys and as part of the IFS family, our reach and impact continue to grow globally.** **You don’t just scope solutions \- you build what others can’t.** As a Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) at Poka, you’ll work directly with leading manufacturers to identify, design, and deliver high\-impact AI and integration solutions inside real operations. This isn’t a traditional solutions or consulting role. You’ll own outcomes end\-to\-end. From discovery through to working software, helping customers move from idea to deployed solution in weeks. Working with Poka’s AI team, who have already designed and built agents, workflows, and prototypes that solve real problems using customer data, systems, and processes. Your role will be bringing AI to life in operational environments and driving acceleration with our global, enterprise customers. **What you’ll be doing** * Partner with customers to deeply understand their operations, systems, and priorities — and identify where AI and integrations can unlock value * Lead discovery sessions and technical discussions, shaping high\-impact use cases and defining realistic solution approaches * Design and build integrations between Poka and enterprise systems (ERP, MES, CMMS, etc.) using APIs and middleware tools * Develop AI\-powered workflows and agents (e.g., operator assistants, intelligent triage, document processing, anomaly detection) using modern LLM frameworks * Own delivery end\-to\-end — from scoping and architecture to implementation, testing, and handoff * Support strategic deals by contributing to pre\-sales conversations, workshops, and technical deep dives * Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and Services to refine solutions and translate field learnings into scalable capabilities * Contribute reusable integration templates and AI workflow patterns that can scale across customers * Travel to customer sites (:25–35%) for workshops, discovery, and key delivery moments **What you bring** * Experience in a customer\-facing technical role (e.g., Solutions Architect, Sales Engineer, Integration Consultant, or similar) * Strong hands\-on experience building AI agents, LLM\-powered workflows, or automation solutions — this is your core strength * Practical experience working with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic) and building real\-world use cases (not just experimentation) * Solid grounding in system integrations, APIs, and connecting enterprise platforms * Ability to quickly go from ambiguous problem → working prototype using tools like Python, Node.js, and modern AI stacks * Confidence bridging business and technical conversations, from frontline operations to IT stakeholders * Strong problem\-solving skills and the ability to adapt solutions to real\-world constraints * Comfort working in ambiguity and helping shape a new, evolving capability within Poka * Familiarity with manufacturing systems or industrial environments is a strong plus **Why Poka** * **Build, don’t just advise** – You own delivery and ship real solutions, not slide decks * **AI with real\-world impact** – Apply cutting\-edge AI in live manufacturing environments, not theoretical use cases * **Speed matters** – Your work goes from idea to deployed solution in weeks * **Shape what comes next** – The solutions you build directly influence Poka’s product roadmap and future AI capabilities * **High\-impact customers** – Work with global manufacturers solving complex, meaningful operational challenges If you’re someone who thrives on building, experimenting, and turning emerging AI capabilities into real\-world impact — this is where that work actually happens. **What we offer:** * Dynamic and collaborative work environment. * Generous vacation policy upon arrival. * Remote work policy. * A truly flexible schedule that allows you to adapt your work hours. * Benefits Plan offered from day one. * MacBook Pro and €500 allowance for your home office. * Generous referral bonus. * The tools, support, and opportunities you need to keep learning and progressing in your career. *Want to learn more about our culture? Click HERE.* **Please note:** An understanding of English is essential at Poka, as it is the language used during company\-wide meetings and many internal communications, ensuring effective collaboration and decision\-making. In addition, as Poka operates in an international environment, English is the primary language for communicating with our clients and partners, enabling us to better serve them and represent Poka in a professional manner.

The successful candidate will demonstrate strong expertise in modern Python development, a solid understanding of the Python ecosystem and tooling, and a passion for building high\-quality, scalable software solutions. Engineers at Propylon have significant influence over technical decision\-making and are encouraged to take ownership of the solutions they design and deliver. Job Overview: **Responsibilities:** * Work with technical team leads to design overall application architecture. * Collaborate with the team to develop and implement custom components that integrate existing systems with Propylon’s TimeArc suite of products. * Drive development across backend components. * Contribute to product architecture and design decisions. * Participate in code reviews and promote engineering best practices. **Requirements:** * Excellent understanding of the Python programming language, its ecosystem and idioms. * 3\+ years of professional experience with a Python\-based MVC framework, preferably Django. * Experience developing application APIs that utilise data and functionality exposed through REST, GraphQL, RPC or similar technologies. * Excellent working knowledge of SQL\-based relational databases, such as PostgreSQL or MySQL. * Experience with modern Python features including type hinting, asynchronous programming and data classes. * Familiarity with Docker and related containerisation technologies. * Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with experience working within cross\-functional teams. **Nice to haves:** * Working knowledge of a modern JavaScript framework, preferably React * Experience with XML processing/transformation. * Experience with document management/document workflow software. * Familiarity or work experience with DevOps workflow/principles, building CI/CD pipelines. **Personal Attributes:** * Wants to be part of a team that is committed to delivering high quality innovative products. * Wants to be part of a culture that is committed to sharing and enhancing the knowledge of the wider team, supporting team wide learning and success * Have a curious mindset – about what you do, the technology you use and how you connect with others to deliver products that we are proud to deliver to our clients. In return, we expect all our people to live by the values that unite us: **to partner**, putting clients fist and winning together**, to pioneer**, innovating fearlessly and leading with vision and courage, **to progress**, aiming high and growing through actions and **to deliver**, owning the outcome and building trust with our colleagues and clients. **RWS embraces DEI** and promotes equal opportunity, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind. title: Graduate Civil Engineer
description: The Colas Group operates internationally in all aspects of transport infrastructure. As part of the global organisation, Colas Ireland is primarily involved the distribution of bitumen products, emulsion and emulsifier manufacturing, and the provision of specialist road maintenance services on the national, regional and local road network. In Ireland Colas operates from 14 locations across the island of Ireland. **Graduate Civil Engineering Programme** **International Graduate Programme – Civil Engineering** **Build Your Global Career with a Market Leader** At **Colas Ireland** , part of the world‑renowned Bouygues Group, we don’t just build roads — we build careers, leaders, and opportunities that span the globe. With over 100 years of excellence and operations in **54 countries** , we are a market leader in delivering innovative, sustainable infrastructure projects. The **Colas GPS Graduate Programme** is a **2‑year, fully funded** international journey for ambitious Civil Engineering graduates ready to work on cutting‑edge projects and fast‑track their careers. **What You’ll Get** * €37,500 starting salary plus potential performance bonus. * 3 rotations across 3 countries – 6 months in Ireland (Dublin, Galway, Ennis or Athlone) followed by 1\.5 years international assignments. * Hands‑on responsibilities that put you at the heart of large‑scale projects. * Training, mentoring, and exposure to senior industry experts. * A guaranteed pathway to a full‑time role on completion. **Your Role** From your first day, you’ll play a key part in managing site operations, tracking project milestones, and collaborating with teams to deliver world‑class infrastructure. Whether it’s bridges, high‑tech facilities, or iconic transport networks, you’ll work on projects that shape communities and make an impact. **Who We’re Looking For** * Recent Civil Engineering graduates with a passion for infrastructure and sustainability. * Adaptable, curious problem‑solvers keen to embrace new cultures and challenges. * Fluent in English (other languages welcome) and willing to travel. **How It Works** Start in Ireland for 6 months, then embark on 1\.5 years fully funded international rotations — gaining invaluable global experience and joining a network of professionals across multiple continents. **This is your chance to join a market leader with a reputation for excellence, international mobility, and a commitment to developing future leaders.** Apply now and start your global journey with Colas Ireland 2026\. *At Colas, our culture is guided by four core values: **Respect** , **Commitment** , **Pioneering** , and **Sharing** . These values shape how we work, collaborate, and grow together. We believe that **respect** is the foundation of every relationship. By treating others as we wish to be treated, we foster fairness, transparency, and inclusion within our teams and with our clients, communities, and partners. **Commitment** means dedicating ourselves fully to everything we do. We strive to create an environment where everyone feels valued, supported, and empowered to make a meaningful impact. With a **pioneering** spirit, we prioritise bold innovation over blind ambition. We welcome new ideas, invest in our people, and work to deliver sustainable infrastructure solutions that shape the future. Through **sharing** , we build trust and solidarity, encouraging collaboration and growth at all levels of the business.* *Colas Group is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce that mirrors the communities we serve. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from all backgrounds. If you want a workplace where your voice is valued and your potential is developed, Colas is the right choice.*

title: Graduate Development Programme
description: Are you an aspiring engineer looking for a supportive environment to grow your career? As one of Ireland’s leading engineering consultancies, we invite you to apply for our Graduate Development Programme. As a fully Irish\-owned firm specializing in the planning, engineering design and delivery in the property, infrastructure, environmental energy sectors, with over 37 years’ experience in the industry, DBFL offers a unique opportunity to kickstart your career. By joining our team, you can become part of our legacy of excellence and innovation, working alongside some of the most skilled professionals in the industry. Our Graduate Development Programme is specifically designed to support your growth from graduate level to Chartership over a period of four years. From the moment you step through our doors, you will be immersed in a dynamic, supportive and collaborative environment that encourages you to learn and excel. You will receive hands\-on experience through a range of exciting projects and benefit from a structured training programme that includes mentorship, internal training sessions, educational site visits, external workshops, and self\-directed learning opportunities. This comprehensive approach ensures that you develop the skills and competencies necessary to thrive in as your engineering career develops. At DBFL, we understand that the journey to becoming a Chartered Engineer is a significant milestone in your professional development. As an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited organization, we are committed to providing you with the resources and support needed to achieve Chartership. Our programme aligns with the current requirements of Engineers Ireland, ensuring that all areas of competency and core skills are thoroughly covered. With our guidance and your dedication, you will be well on your way to obtaining this prestigious professional title within a minimum of four years. We believe that a successful career is built not only on technical expertise but also on a supportive and inclusive work environment. With a Silver Investors in Diversity accreditation from The Irish Centre for Diversity, DBFL is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, fostering a culture where every team member is valued and heard. Our modern, open\-plan offices in all our locations, promote collaboration and innovation, and our active social calendar ensures that you will have plenty of opportunities to build strong professional relationships and enjoy your time at DBFL. We strive to create a workplace where you can thrive both professionally and personally. To be considered for the programme you will need the following: * Bachelor’s or Masters’ Degree in Civil /Environmental Engineering * A good understanding of civil principles * Good written and oral communication skills * Excellent analytical, critical thinking and strategic skills * Good standards of accuracy and attention to detail * Good Computer skills * ‘A looking to learn’ attitude * Works well on own initiative and as part of a team Are you ready to take the first step towards an exciting and fulfilling career with DBFL? To be considered for our Graduate Development Programme, you should have a Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Civil, Structural or Transportation Engineering, strong analytical and critical thinking skills, and a keen eye for detail. We value good communication skills and a proactive, team\-oriented attitude. Join us at DBFL, where your engineering journey begins, and where you can become part of our mission, Engineering Sustainable Futures.

title: Graduate Mechanical Engineer
description: Graduate Mechanical Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now ***Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.*** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Co\-ordinating with site teams * Attend daily/ weekly meetings as required * Prepare reports/presentations as required * Assisting Intermediate and Senior Engineers * Ensure design complies with project specifications * Prepare technical submittals in accordance with project specifications and procurement schedule * Ensure materials / plant ordered are approved and in compliance with specification. * Work with BIM to develop construction plan and work packaging **Essential** **Selection Criteria / Person Specification:** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree * Good communication skills * Strong interpersonal and organisational skills * Adaptable and solutions driven * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Good organization \& co\-ordination skills * Flexible to travel * Enthusiastic and energetic **Desirable** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** Apply Now

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? Want to travel, gain international work experience, and build transferable skills while earning a competitive salary? **English 1** is hiring **recent graduates and early\-career professionals** to teach English in China. No prior teaching experience required. Full training, visa sponsorship, and structured career development are included. This is an ideal **first job** , **graduate program alternative** , or **gap\-year career launch** for ambitious, globally minded graduates. **What you'll do** * Teach English to young learners aged 3–16 in a fun, interactive, and immersive classroom setting. * Deliver high\-energy lessons using English 1’s award\-winning curriculum and digital tools. * Provide constructive feedback and help students build confidence in their English skills. * Take part in school events, cultural activities, and student engagement programs. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary \+ performance bonus after 6 months * 21 days paid leave (10 days paid leave \+ 11 days statutory holidays) * Flight allowance * International health insurance (co\-paid) * Airport pick\-up support * \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa \+ up to 2 weeks sponsored hotel stay * Comprehensive induction training, ongoing support, and fully prepared teaching materials * TEFL certificate \+ skill development \& career advancement * Distance learning courses (Level 6 Trinity College CertPT, DELTM) * English 1\-organized social and cultural activities \+ free Mandarin Chinese lessons *The hotel accommodation for the first two weeks will be deemed as a sponsorship upon successful completion of the first\-year contract. \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa expenses will be reimbursed.* **Who we’re looking for** * A bachelor’s degree (in any subject) * A valid passport * A genuine interest in education, travel, and cultural exchange. * ESL certification (TEFL, TESOL, CELTA) is a plus\- we can sponsor * Clean background check. **Why work at English 1?** * Teachers are at the core of everything we do at English 1 * Over **30 years** of success in China with **200\+ schools** in **50\+ cities** . * Strong presence in **China** and **Indonesia** . * **Professional development** and career **advancement opportunities** .
